name: New Orleans (United States)

colors.symbolism: the three fleurs-de-lis — emblem of the French heritage

description: A white field bordered above by a red horizontal stripe and below by a blue horizontal stripe, charged with three gold fleurs-de-lis arranged horizontally across the centre.
history: Adopted in January 1918 — selected by a Citizens' Flag Committee from nearly 400 submissions in preparation for the bicentennial of the city's 1718 founding. The patriotic colour scheme was suggested by engraver Bernard Barry; the fleur-de-lis idea is attributed to draftsman Gustave Couret. The flag was first raised over Gallier Hall, then the city hall, on 9 February 1918. The colours match the French Tricolour and the fleurs-de-lis recall the city's origins as a French colonial foundation under the Sieur de Bienville and the long French and Spanish heritage of Louisiana.

features.symbolism: a thin red stripe above and a thin blue stripe below frame a wide white field; in heraldic interpretation white symbolises justice and government, blue liberty and red fraternity — also the colours of the French Tricolour

features.role: three gold fleurs-de-lis on the white field
features.symbolism: the fleur-de-lis, traditional French royal emblem, recalls New Orleans's foundation by Jean-Baptiste Le Moyne, Sieur de Bienville in 1718, the city's long French heritage, and its subsequent Spanish period (a fleur-de-lis is a Spanish heraldic charge as well, brought to Spain by the Bourbon dynasty)
